# Code of Conduct

## The short version

**Be decent.** Assume good faith. Give useful, specific feedback. Don't be a jerk.

## The slightly longer version

These are small, community-facing modding projects. Everyone here — the maintainer, contributors, players filing bugs — is doing this in their spare time. Interactions should reflect that.

### Expected

- Communicate with respect, even in disagreement
- Give concrete, actionable feedback ("the reactor block crashes when X" beats "this is broken")
- Assume the other person has good intent unless there's clear evidence otherwise
- Credit others' work when you build on it (per the MIT license)

### Not expected

- Personal attacks, harassment, or discrimination of any kind (race, gender, sexuality, ability, nationality, religion, age, appearance — any of it)
- Hostile or dismissive language in issues, PRs, or discussions
- Doxxing or sharing anyone's personal info
- Off-topic political flamewars in mod issues

### Enforcement

If someone violates this in an issue, PR, or discussion:

1. First response: a warning, and usually the offending comment gets edited or removed
2. Repeat or severe violation: temporary or permanent ban from the repo, and if warranted, escalation to GitHub

The maintainer ([Godimas101](https://github.com/Godimas101)) has final say. Report violations by:

- Opening a private security advisory on the affected repo, OR
- DMing on Patreon: [patreon.com/Godimas101](https://patreon.com/Godimas101)

Adapted loosely from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), but kept small and specific to this community.
